member.name: T:Bonsai.Harp.AsyncDevice
member.summary: Represents an asynchronous API to configure and interface with Harp devices.

member.name: M:Bonsai.Harp.AsyncDevice.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class on the specified port.
member.param: The name of the serial port used to communicate with the Harp device.

member.summary: Asynchronously reads the identity class of the device.
member.param: A which can be used to cancel the operation.
member.returns: A task that represents the asynchronous read operation. The property contains the identity class of the device.

A which can be used to cancel the operation.

member.name: M:Bonsai.Harp.AsyncDevice.Dispose

member.name: T:Bonsai.Harp.Bootloader
member.summary: Provides asynchronous operations to update a device firmware using the dedicated bootloader protocol.

member.name: M:Bonsai.Harp.Bootloader.UpdateFirmwareAsync(System.String,Bonsai.Harp.DeviceFirmware,System.IProgress{System.Int32})
member.summary: Asynchronously updates the firmware of the Harp device on the specified port.
member.param: The name of the serial port used to communicate with the Harp device.
The binary firmware image to upload to the device.
The optional object used to report update progress.
member.returns: The task object representing the asynchronous firmware update operation.

member.name: M:Bonsai.Harp.Bootloader.UpdateFirmwareAsync(System.String,Bonsai.Harp.DeviceFirmware,System.Boolean,System.IProgress{System.Int32})
member.summary: Asynchronously updates the firmware of the Harp device on the specified port.
member.param: The name of the serial port used to communicate with the Harp device.
The binary firmware image to upload to the device.

true to indicate that the firmware should be uploaded even if the device reports unsupported hardware, or is in bootloader mode; false to throw an exception if the firmware is not supported, or the device is in an invalid state.
The optional object used to report update progress.
member.returns: The task object representing the asynchronous firmware update operation.

member.name: T:Bonsai.Harp.CreateTimestamped
member.summary: Represents an operator that creates a sequence of timestamped payload values from a sequence of value-timestamp pairs.

member.name: M:Bonsai.Harp.CreateTimestamped.Process``1(System.IObservable{System.Tuple{``0,System.Double}})
member.summary: Creates an observable sequence of timestamped payload values from a sequence of value-timestamp pairs.
member.typeparam: The type of the value in the timestamped payload.
member.param: A sequence of value-timestamp pairs, where the second element specifies the timestamp of the payload, in fractional seconds.
member.returns: An observable sequence of timestamped payload values.

member.name: M:Bonsai.Harp.CreateTimestamped.Process``1(System.IObservable{System.Tuple{``0,Bonsai.Harp.HarpMessage}})
member.summary: Creates an observable sequence of timestamped payload values from a sequence of value-message pairs.
member.typeparam: The type of the value in the timestamped payload.
member.param: A sequence of value-message pairs, where the second element is a specifying the timestamp of the payload, in fractional seconds.
member.returns: An observable sequence of timestamped payload values.

member.name: M:Bonsai.Harp.CreateTimestamped.Process(System.IObservable{Bonsai.Harp.HarpMessage})
member.summary: Creates an observable sequence of timestamped message values surfacing the timestamp of the message object in fractional seconds.
member.param: A sequence of timestamped objects.
member.returns: An observable sequence of timestamped values representing the original message and its timestamp, in fractional seconds.

member.name: M:Bonsai.Harp.CreateTimestamped.Process``1(System.IObservable{System.Reactive.Timestamped{``0}})
member.summary: Creates an observable sequence of timestamped message values by converting the timestamp in the local scheduler clock to a Harp timestamp, in fractional seconds relative to the reference time.
member.typeparam: The type of the value in the timestamped payload.
member.param: A sequence of values specifying the timestamp in a local scheduler clock.
member.returns: An observable sequence of Harp timestamped payload values.

member.name: T:Bonsai.Harp.ResetFlags
member.summary: Specifies the behavior of the non-volatile registers when resetting the device.

member.name: F:Bonsai.Harp.ResetFlags.None
member.summary: All reset flags are cleared.

member.name: F:Bonsai.Harp.ResetFlags.RestoreDefault
member.summary: The device will boot with all the registers reset to their default factory values.

member.name: F:Bonsai.Harp.ResetFlags.RestoreEeprom
member.summary: The device will boot and restore all the registers to the values stored in non-volatile memory.

member.name: F:Bonsai.Harp.ResetFlags.Save
member.summary: The device will boot and save all the current register values to non-volatile memory.

member.name: F:Bonsai.Harp.ResetFlags.RestoreName
member.summary: The device will boot with the default device name.

member.name: F:Bonsai.Harp.ResetFlags.BootFromDefault
member.summary: Specifies that the device has booted from default factory values.

member.name: F:Bonsai.Harp.ResetFlags.BootFromEeprom
member.summary: Specifies that the device has booted from non-volatile values stored in EEPROM.

member.name: T:Bonsai.Harp.ClockConfigurationFlags
member.summary: Specifies configuration flags for the device synchronization clock.

member.name: F:Bonsai.Harp.ClockConfigurationFlags.None
member.summary: All clock configuration flags are cleared.

member.name: F:Bonsai.Harp.ClockConfigurationFlags.ClockRepeater
member.summary: The device will repeat the clock synchronization signal to the clock output connector, if available.

member.name: F:Bonsai.Harp.ClockConfigurationFlags.ClockGenerator
member.summary: The device resets and generates the clock synchronization signal on the clock output connector, if available.

member.name: F:Bonsai.Harp.ClockConfigurationFlags.RepeaterCapability
member.summary: Specifies the device has the capability to repeat the clock synchronization signal to the clock output connector.

member.name: F:Bonsai.Harp.ClockConfigurationFlags.GeneratorCapability
member.summary: Specifies the device has the capability to generate the clock synchronization signal to the clock output connector.

member.name: F:Bonsai.Harp.ClockConfigurationFlags.ClockUnlock
member.summary: The device will unlock the timestamp register counter and will accept commands to set new timestamp values.

member.name: F:Bonsai.Harp.ClockConfigurationFlags.ClockLock
member.summary: The device will lock the timestamp register counter and will not accept commands to set new timestamp values.

member.name: T:Bonsai.Harp.OperationMode
member.summary: Specifies the operation mode of the device.

member.name: F:Bonsai.Harp.OperationMode.Standby
member.summary: Disable all event reporting on the device.

member.name: F:Bonsai.Harp.OperationMode.Active
member.summary: Event detection is enabled. Only enabled events are reported by the device.

member.name: F:Bonsai.Harp.OperationMode.Speed
member.summary: The device enters speed mode.

member.name: T:Bonsai.Harp.EnableFlag
member.summary: Specifies whether a specific register flag is enabled or disabled.

member.name: F:Bonsai.Harp.EnableFlag.Disabled
member.summary: Specifies that the flag is disabled.

member.name: F:Bonsai.Harp.EnableFlag.Enabled
member.summary: Specifies that the flag is enabled.

member.name: T:Bonsai.Harp.DeviceRegisters
member.summary: Provides address and payload types for common registers and operation control of all Harp devices.

member.name: F:Bonsai.Harp.DeviceRegisters.WhoAmI
member.summary: The address of the WHO_AM_I register used to verify the identity class of the device. This field is read-only.

member.name: F:Bonsai.Harp.DeviceRegisters.HardwareVersionHigh
member.summary: The address of the HW_VERSION_H register containing the major hardware version of the device. This field is read-only.

member.name: F:Bonsai.Harp.DeviceRegisters.HardwareVersionLow
member.summary: The address of the HW_VERSION_L register containing the minor hardware version of the device. This field is read-only.

member.name: F:Bonsai.Harp.DeviceRegisters.AssemblyVersion
member.summary: The address of the ASSEMBLY_VERSION register containing the version of the assembled components in the device. This field is read-only.

member.name: F:Bonsai.Harp.DeviceRegisters.CoreVersionHigh
member.summary: The address of the HARP_VERSION_H register containing the major version of the Harp core used by the device. This field is read-only.

member.name: F:Bonsai.Harp.DeviceRegisters.CoreVersionLow
member.summary: The address of the HARP_VERSION_L register containing the minor version of the Harp core used by the device. This field is read-only.

member.name: F:Bonsai.Harp.DeviceRegisters.FirmwareVersionHigh
member.summary: The address of the FW_VERSION_H register containing the major firmware version installed in the device. This field is read-only.

member.name: F:Bonsai.Harp.DeviceRegisters.FirmwareVersionLow
member.summary: The address of the FW_VERSION_L register containing the minor firmware version installed in the device. This field is read-only.

member.name: F:Bonsai.Harp.DeviceRegisters.TimestampSecond
member.summary: The address of the TIMESTAMP_SECOND register containing the integral part of the system timestamp, in seconds. This field is read-only.

member.name: F:Bonsai.Harp.DeviceRegisters.TimestampMicrosecond
member.summary: The address of the TIMESTAMP_MICRO register containing the fractional part of the system timestamp, in microseconds. This field is read-only.

member.name: F:Bonsai.Harp.DeviceRegisters.OperationControl

member.name: F:Bonsai.Harp.DeviceRegisters.ResetDevice
member.summary: The address of the RESET_DEV register used to reset the device and save non-volatile registers. This field is read-only.

member.name: F:Bonsai.Harp.DeviceRegisters.DeviceName
member.summary: The address of the DEVICE_NAME register containing the user configurable name for the device. This field is read-only.

member.name: T:Bonsai.Harp.FilterHarpMessage
member.summary: Represents an operator which filters a sequence of Harp messages for elements that match the specified address and message type.

member.summary: Represents an operator that filters the sequence for Harp messages matching the specified address and message type.

member.name: P:Bonsai.Harp.FilterMessage.MessageType
member.summary: Gets or sets a value specifying the expected message type. This parameter is optional.

member.name: P:Bonsai.Harp.FilterMessage.Address
member.summary: Gets or sets the expected message address. This parameter is optional.

member.name: M:Bonsai.Harp.FilterMessage.Process(System.IObservable{Bonsai.Harp.HarpMessage})
member.summary: Returns an observable sequence of Harp messages matching the specified address and message type.
member.param: An observable sequence of Harp messages.
member.returns: An observable sequence of Harp messages matching the specified address and message type. If message type or address are , messages of any type or from any address, respectively, are accepted.

member.name: T:Bonsai.Harp.FilterMessageType
member.summary: Represents an operator that filters the sequence for valid Harp messages matching the specified message type.

member.summary: Gets or sets a value specifying how the message filter will use the matching criteria.

member.name: P:Bonsai.Harp.FilterMessageType.MessageType
member.summary: Gets or sets a value specifying the expected message type. If no value is specified, all messages will be accepted.

member.name: M:Bonsai.Harp.FilterMessageType.Process(System.IObservable{Bonsai.Harp.HarpMessage})
member.summary: Filters an observable sequence for Harp messages matching the specified message type criteria.
member.param: An observable sequence of Harp messages.
member.returns: An observable sequence including or excluding the Harp messages matching the specified message type, depending on the specified filter type. If message type is , messages of any type are accepted. Error messages are always rejected.

member.name: T:Bonsai.Harp.FirmwareMetadata
member.summary: Represents information about the device, firmware version and hardware version numbers contained in a particular device or hex file.

member.name: M:Bonsai.Harp.FirmwareMetadata.#ctor(System.String,Bonsai.Harp.HarpVersion,Bonsai.Harp.HarpVersion,Bonsai.Harp.HarpVersion,System.Nullable{System.Int32},System.Nullable{System.Int32})
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class with the specified device name, the firmware version and compatible hardware versions.
member.param: The unique identifier of the device type on which the firmware should be installed.
The version of the firmware contained in the device or hex file.
The version of the Harp core implemented by the firmware.
The hardware version of the device, or range of hardware versions supported by the firmware.
The board assembly version of the device, or range of assembly versions supported by the firmware.
The optional prerelease number, for preview versions of the firmware.

member.summary: Gets the board assembly version of the device, or range of assembly versions supported by the firmware.

member.summary: Specifies the behavior of the non-volatile registers when resetting the device.

member.summary: The device will boot with all the registers reset to their default factory values.

member.name: F:Bonsai.Harp.ResetMode.RestoreEeprom
member.summary: The device will boot and restore all the registers to the values stored in non-volatile memory.

member.name: F:Bonsai.Harp.ResetMode.Save
member.summary: The device will boot and save all the current register values to non-volatile memory.

member.name: F:Bonsai.Harp.ResetMode.RestoreName
member.summary: The device will boot with the default device name.

member.name: T:Bonsai.Harp.OffsetTimestamp
member.summary: Represents an operator that shifts the timestamps of a sequence of timestamped payload values by the specified time interval.

member.name: P:Bonsai.Harp.OffsetTimestamp.TimeShift
member.summary: Gets or sets a time interval by which to offset the sequence timestamps.

member.name: P:Bonsai.Harp.OffsetTimestamp.TimeShiftXml
member.summary: Gets or sets an XML representation of the offset time interval for serialization.

member.name: M:Bonsai.Harp.OffsetTimestamp.Process``1(System.IObservable{Bonsai.Harp.Timestamped{``0}})
member.summary: Shifts the timestamps of an observable sequence of timestamped payload values by the specified offset.
member.typeparam: The type of the values in each timestamped payload.
member.param: An observable sequence of timestamped payload values.
member.returns: A sequence of timestamped payload values where each timestamp is computed by adding the time interval offset to the timestamp of the corresponding payload in the sequence.

member.name: M:Bonsai.Harp.OffsetTimestamp.Process``1(System.IObservable{System.Tuple{Bonsai.Harp.Timestamped{``0},System.Double}})
member.summary: Shifts the timestamps of an observable sequence of timestamped payload values by the specified offset, in fractional seconds.
member.typeparam: The type of the values in each timestamped payload.
member.param: A sequence of pairs containing a timestamped payload and a time interval, in fractional seconds, by which to offset the payload timestamp.
member.returns: A sequence of timestamped payload values where each timestamp is computed by adding both the seconds offset and the base offset to the timestamp of the corresponding payload in the sequence.

member.name: M:Bonsai.Harp.OffsetTimestamp.Process``1(System.IObservable{System.Tuple{Bonsai.Harp.Timestamped{``0},System.TimeSpan}})
member.summary: Shifts the timestamps of an observable sequence of timestamped payload values by the specified time interval.
member.typeparam: The type of the values in each timestamped payload.
member.param: A sequence of pairs containing a timestamped payload and the time interval by which to offset the payload timestamp.
member.returns: A sequence of timestamped payload values where each timestamp is computed by adding both the time interval and the base offset to the timestamp of the corresponding payload in the sequence.
